_meta.description: Three demo personas, each backed by a REAL wobbly-donkey customer harvested live from Bloomreach Engagement via the loomi MCP (list_customer_events, 2026-05-27). Friendly display names (Elva / Marvin / Jaylon) front the real customers; persona_id is kept stable (guest/sarah/alex) so the app, boost rules and cached-results filenames keep working. All personas use demo_query: 'necklace'. Sandbox emails are NEVER shown in the UI — see real_customer.identifier for provenance only.
_meta.source: Bloomreach Engagement (loomi MCP) — list_customer_events on project wobbly-donkey

personas.profile_description: Real customer with just 2 lifetime events — a push-notification banner impression and a registration, both on 7 Oct 2025. A brand-new account with essentially zero behavioural signal, and no persistent BRUID, so Discovery cannot recognise her across sessions or personalise her results yet.
personas.plp_before_state: Bestsellers at top — generic ranking, no personalisation active
personas.plp_after_state: Bestsellers at top — Rule 3 active (is_bestseller boost for New Prospecting segment)
personas.personalisation_gap: With only a registration and one banner impression — and no BRUID — Bloomreach has near-zero signal and cannot identify Elva across sessions. Rule 3 targets the segment, but a persistent BRUID is required for cross-session recognition.

journey.detail: Push notifications subscription banner (Variant A) shown

personas.profile_description: Real customer in Denver, Colorado with 47 lifetime events. His most recent product view was a Cartier 'C de Cartier wedding band' ($2,680) on 29 Aug 2025 — a classic gift — which he has not purchased. He is an active buyer (prior purchases include a $17.99 DressBerry trouser and a $32,700 Cartier 'Love bracelet, diamond-paved') and engages with newsletters and onsite banners. BRUID present, so Discovery can recognise and segment him.
personas.plp_before_state: Generic ranking — Rule 1 INACTIVE, gift products buried
personas.plp_after_state: Gift sets at top — Rule 1 active (gift_eligible boost for Gifting Intent segment)
personas.personalisation_gap: BRUID present and Marvin is researching a high-end gift (the Cartier wedding band) without converting on it. Pre-fix: Rule 1 inactive, so gift-eligible products stay buried. Post-fix: Rule 1 active and gift products surface at the top of his results.

journey.detail: Cartier 'C de Cartier wedding band' — $2,680 (not purchased)
'Onexit banner with last viewed items' — closed

personas.profile_description: Real customer and returning, identified shopper. Completed a $186.98 purchase (LUXURAZI suit) via Apple Pay on 23 May 2025, earned Gold loyalty status (210 pts), and left a positive post-purchase survey ('I love my purchase'). BRUID present with a complete, recent funnel (browse → cart → checkout → purchase) — the gold-standard personalisation contrast against Elva.
personas.plp_before_state: Generic ranking — Rule 2 INACTIVE, premium/new arrivals not surfaced
personas.plp_after_state: Premium and new arrivals at top — Rule 2 active (is_new_arrival OR price_band=premium boost for High Value Returning segment)
personas.personalisation_gap: Strong BRUID + completed purchase + loyalty membership + a positive survey. Pre-fix: Rule 2 inactive. Post-fix: full 1:1 personalisation active — premium and new-arrival products surface at the top.

journey.detail: LUXURAZI Men Grey Checked Slim Fit Suit — $186.98
'I love my purchase. Delivery could be faster though.'
